eastern_philosophers.name: 孔子 (Confucius)
core_ideas: 仁 (Rén) – Benevolence / Humaneness
禮 (Lǐ) – Ritual propriety
義 (Yì) – Righteousness
君子 (Jūnzǐ) – The noble person as moral ideal
正名 (Zhèngmíng) – Rectification of names
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Confucianism; shaped Chinese, Korean, Japanese, and Vietnamese ethics, politics, and education for over two millennia.

eastern_philosophers.name: 老子 (Laozi)
core_ideas: 道 (Dào) – The Way as the fundamental principle of the universe
無為 (Wúwéi) – Non-action / effortless action
自然 (Zìrán) – Naturalness, spontaneity
柔弱勝剛強 – Softness overcomes hardness
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founding figure of Taoism; profoundly influenced Chinese philosophy, religion, arts, and statecraft.

eastern_philosophers.name: 莊子 (Zhuangzi)
core_ideas: 相對主義 – Relativism of perspectives
齊物論 – Equalisation of all things
逍遙遊 – Free and easy wandering
蝴蝶夢 – The butterfly dream (nature of reality and self)
eastern_philosophers.influence: Developed and deepened Taoist philosophy; inspired Chan/Zen Buddhism and Chinese literary tradition.

eastern_philosophers.name: 孟子 (Mencius)
core_ideas: 性善論 – Human nature is inherently good
仁政 – Benevolent government
民為貴 – The people are most important (proto-democratic thought)
四端 – Four moral sprouts (compassion, shame, modesty, moral judgment)
eastern_philosophers.influence: Called the 'Second Sage' of Confucianism; his optimistic view of human nature became orthodox Neo-Confucian doctrine.

eastern_philosophers.name: 荀子 (Xunzi)
core_ideas: 性惡論 – Human nature is inherently bad; goodness is acquired
禮義之化 – Moral transformation through ritual and righteousness
天人之分 – Separation of Heaven and human agency
名學 – Theory of names and language
eastern_philosophers.influence: Synthesised Confucian and Legalist ideas; influenced Han Feizi and the early Han dynasty's statecraft.

eastern_philosophers.name: 墨子 (Mozi)
core_ideas: 兼愛 (Jiān'ài) – Universal love / impartial caring
非攻 – Opposition to aggressive warfare
尚賢 – Meritocracy
節用 – Frugality
天志 – Will of Heaven as moral standard
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Mohism, the main rival to Confucianism in the pre-Qin era; pioneered early formal logic and scientific thinking in China.

eastern_philosophers.name: 韓非子 (Han Feizi)
core_ideas: 法 (Fǎ) – Law as the basis of governance
術 (Shù) – Administrative techniques
勢 (Shì) – Positional power of the ruler
性惡 – Pessimistic view of human nature
eastern_philosophers.influence: Synthesised Legalist thought; directly shaped the Qin dynasty's unification of China and continues to influence political realism.

eastern_philosophers.name: 朱熹 (Zhu Xi)
key_works: 四書集注 (Collected Commentaries on the Four Books)
近思錄 (Reflections on Things at Hand)
core_ideas: 理 (Lǐ) – Principle / Pattern underlying all things
氣 (Qì) – Vital force / material energy
格物致知 – Investigation of things to extend knowledge
太極 – The Supreme Ultimate
eastern_philosophers.influence: Systematised Neo-Confucianism; his commentaries became the official curriculum for imperial examinations in China, Korea, and Japan.

eastern_philosophers.name: 王陽明 (Wang Yangming)
key_works: 大學問 (Inquiry on the Great Learning)
core_ideas: 心即理 – Mind is principle
致良知 – Extension of innate moral knowledge
知行合一 – Unity of knowledge and action
心外無物 – Nothing exists outside the mind
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded the Yangming school; enormously influential in Ming China, Joseon Korea, Tokugawa Japan, and the modern New Confucian movement.

eastern_philosophers.name: 悉達多·喬達摩 (Siddhartha Gautama / The Buddha)
eastern_philosophers.born: c. 480 BCE (traditional: c. 563 BCE; dates are debated in scholarship)
eastern_philosophers.died: c. 400 BCE (traditional: c. 483 BCE; dates are debated in scholarship)
core_ideas: 四聖諦 – The Four Noble Truths (suffering, its origin, its cessation, the path)
八正道 – The Noble Eightfold Path
無常 (Anicca) – Impermanence
無我 (Anattā) – Non-self
緣起 (Pratītyasamutpāda) – Dependent origination
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Buddhism, one of the world's major religions and philosophical systems, which spread throughout Asia and now globally.

eastern_philosophers.name: 龍樹 (Nāgārjuna)
core_ideas: 空性 (Śūnyatā) – Emptiness; all phenomena lack inherent existence
中道 – The Middle Way between existence and non-existence
二諦 – Two truths (conventional and ultimate)
Prasaṅga – Reductio ad absurdum as philosophical method
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Madhyamaka; considered the 'Second Buddha'; foundational to Mahāyāna, Tibetan, Chan/Zen, and Huayan Buddhism.

eastern_philosophers.name: 商羯羅 (Adi Shankaracharya)
core_ideas: 不二論 (Advaita) – Non-dualism: Brahman alone is real
梵我一如 – Ātman (self) is identical to Brahman (ultimate reality)
幻 (Māyā) – The world of multiplicity is illusion
解脫 (Moksha) – Liberation through knowledge (jñāna)
eastern_philosophers.influence: Consolidated Advaita Vedanta as the dominant school of Hindu philosophy; revived Hinduism against Buddhist influence in India.

eastern_philosophers.name: 羅摩奴闍 (Rāmānuja)
key_works: Sri Bhashya (Commentary on the Brahma Sutras)
core_ideas: 限定不二論 (Vishishtadvaita) – Qualified non-dualism
個我與梵同一而有別 – Individual souls and the world are real but form the body of Brahman
虔信 (Bhakti) – Devotion as the path to liberation
恩典 (Prapatti) – Self-surrender to God
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Vishishtadvaita Vedanta; shaped Vaishnavism and the Sri Vaishnava tradition; a major counterpoint to Shankara.

eastern_philosophers.name: 道元 (Dōgen Zenji)
core_ideas: 只管打坐 (Shikantaza) – Just sitting; zazen as itself enlightenment
修證一等 – Practice and enlightenment are one
有時 (Uji) – Being-time: existence is time
山水經 – Mountains and rivers as expressions of Buddha-nature
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded Sōtō Zen in Japan; considered the greatest Japanese philosopher; profoundly influenced Heidegger studies and comparative philosophy.

eastern_philosophers.name: 西田幾多郎 (Nishida Kitarō)
key_works: 善の研究 (An Inquiry into the Good)
場所の論理 (The Logic of Place)
core_ideas: 純粋経験 – Pure experience as the foundation of reality
場所 (Basho) – Place / Topos: absolute nothingness as the locus of all being
絶対無 – Absolute nothingness
自覚 – Self-awareness as the structure of reality
eastern_philosophers.influence: Founded the Kyoto School; first major Japanese philosopher to synthesise Eastern and Western thought using Western academic methods.

eastern_philosophers.name: 李滉 / 退溪 (Yi Hwang / Toegye)
key_works: 聖學十圖 (Ten Diagrams on Sage Learning)
core_ideas: 理發說 – Principle (Li) has its own active agency
四端七情論 – Four beginnings and seven emotions debate
敬 (Gyeong) – Reverence/mindfulness as the foundation of self-cultivation
eastern_philosophers.influence: The most revered Korean Confucian philosopher; his thought shaped Joseon Korea's intellectual culture and influenced Tokugawa Japan.

eastern_philosophers.name: 李珥 / 栗谷 (Yi I / Yulgok)
key_works: 聖學輯要 (Essential Teachings of the Sages)
core_ideas: 氣發理乘說 – Qi is what activates; Li rides Qi
理通氣局 – Principle is universally pervasive; Qi is locally particularised
誠 (Seong) – Sincerity / authenticity as the highest virtue
eastern_philosophers.influence: The leading rival to Toegye; shaped the Kiho school of Korean Confucianism and influenced modern Korean philosophy and education.
